19. Any contract of sale (agreement to sell) which is not 
a registered deed of conveyance (deed of sale) would fall 
short of the requirements of Sections 54 and 55 of the TP 
Act and will not confer any title nor transfer any interest in 
an immovable property (except to the limited right granted 
under Section 53-A of the TP Act). According to the TP 
Act, an agreement of sale, whether with possession or 
without possession, is not a conveyance. Section 54 of 
the TP Act enacts that sale of immovable property can be 
made only by a registered instrument and an agreement 
of sale does not create any interest or charge on its 
subject-matter.”
17. In the instant matter, undisputedly plaintiff claims that there is only an 
agreement to sell, and there is no sale deed executed in his favour by 
the father. As per the settled position of law, this document does not
